name: Okra (Ladies Fingers) Curry
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the okra and dry them on absorbent kitchen paper. Cut into 2.5cm lengths.
2. Heat 3 tablespoons oil in a pan and fry the okra for about 2 minutes, stirring gently until all the oil has been absorbed. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. Blend the coconut with the chilli powder, turmeric, and cumin in a blender, adding 1 tablespoon water to make a smooth paste. Mix this into the yoghurt.
4. Heat the remaining oil in a pan and when hot, add the mustard seeds and red chilli. Let them sizzle for a few seconds until all the seeds have popped, then add the okra.
5. Turn down the heat and add the yoghurt mix. Cook gently for 2-3 minutes, stirring to prevent the yoghurt from separating, until hot through. Add the coriander and curry leaves, and salt to taste.
